description: Chapter-by-chapter validation checkpoints for the DocBook implementation guide.

chapters.title: Introduction to SaxonForms
review_prompts: Is the terminology consistent with implementation files and tests?
Do the use-cases match developer and QA workflows?

chapters.title: SaxonForms and SaxonJS
review_prompts: Are subsystem boundaries clear and consistent across diagrams?
Are one-hop DOM/JS interactions represented explicitly?

chapters.title: Processing Model (Ch. 4)
review_prompts: Does the lifecycle sequence match runtime event dispatch order?
Are chapter statements traceable to assertions in ch04 and [SEQTRACE]-based sequence tests?

chapters.title: Data Types and MIPs (Ch. 5-6)
review_prompts: Are type-coercion and MIP propagation claims backed by chapter tests?

chapters.title: XPath and Controls (Ch. 7-9)

validation.targeted_test_command: npm run test:e2e:static -- tests/xforms/w3c/ch07.spec.ts tests/xforms/w3c/ch08.spec.ts tests/xforms/w3c/ch09.spec.ts

review_prompts: Do XPath mapping details reflect current function rewrite behavior?
Are control mappings and edge cases aligned with chapter tests?

chapters.title: Actions and Submission (Ch. 10-11)

validation.targeted_test_command: npm run test:e2e:static -- tests/xforms/w3c/ch10.spec.ts tests/xforms/w3c/ch11.spec.ts tests/xforms/w3c/sequence-trace.spec.ts

review_prompts: Does action context/scoping behavior match implementation and tests?
Does submission flow coverage include serialization and response-update semantics with [SEQTRACE] template evidence?

chapters.title: Conformance
review_prompts: Do conformance claims match latest gate/baseline evidence?
Are implementation-defined behaviors documented with test impact?

chapters.title: Additional Topics
review_prompts: Are reusable component and accessibility sections concrete enough for implementation work?
Are BPMN references aligned with current workflow diagrams?
